notes: first_agent also beats random 0.830, so the edge is consistency-over-random, not this heuristic specifically
103/200, 95% CI ~[0.45,0.58] includes 0.5: no measurable edge over take-first

Search does NOT beat the board-aware heuristic yet: 0.460 (CI [0.391,0.529], within noise of a tie, point estimate behind). v0 uses DEFAULT rollout policy + crude linear eval; heuristic always takes lethal/goes-first. Next lever: heuristic-as-rollout-policy + always-take-confirmed-lethal, then tune eval.

Full real loop built+robust. Value PREDICTION strong: GBM AUC 0.774 (was logistic 0.663), export verified to match sklearn within 1e-16, sane feature importances, no sign-flips. PLAY: robust search_v vs heuristic 0.485 (progression 0.347 logistic -> 0.420 tree -> 0.485 tree+robust); robust hand search vs heuristic 0.515; search_v vs hand search 0.467. ALL approaches (first/heuristic/hand-search/learned-search) cluster ~0.50 on this deck. KEY LESSON: high global AUC did NOT convert to better play because (a) a tree is piecewise-constant so it ranks NEARBY candidate leaves poorly (search needs local ranking, not global classification), (b) the simple deck has little to exploit so policies converge (user predicted this). Robustness fixes (aggressive opp reply + K-determ averaging) lifted both searches to parity. Method is built and sound; payoff is long-term (complex decks), not this deck.

Combine v1 = PARITY vs heuristic (0.506 CI[0.472,0.541]). vs hand search 0.480 CI[0.431,0.529]; vs first 0.525 CI[0.476,0.573] -- WORSE than pure hand search's 0.585 vs first, i.e. blending the MC-trained value DILUTES the hand eval's edge. The blend recovered the learned value from 0.427 to parity but adds nothing on this simple deck; lowering lambda just recovers hand search. Tuning the blend weight will not help: the FIX is a better value (search-bootstrapped targets, plan step 2) and/or a complex deck with signal. Best shippable agent remains agent_search (hand eval).

Bootstrap pass 1 (hand-eval leaf targets). search_v vs heuristic 0.517 CI[0.469,0.566] (was 0.427 with MC-outcome value); vs hand search 0.527 CI[0.470,0.582] (was 0.467); combine vs heuristic 0.477; combine vs first 0.557 CI[0.509,0.605]. The search-bootstrapped target fixed the local-ranking loss: search_v no longer loses to the heuristic. All-but-one CI include 0.5 (parity). Pass 1 imitates the hand search (its leaf eval was the hand eval), so it matches but does not exceed it. Caught+fixed a target-saturation bug: raw targets included terminal +-1e6/scale = +-500 logits (21% of rows) collapsing the value to near-binary; clipping the target logit to +-6 restored a gradient (66% mid-range, Pearson 0.825). NEXT: pass 2 = put the learned value at the search leaves, regenerate targets, retrain (expert-iteration loop).

Loop pass 2 (learned-value leaves). search_v vs heuristic 0.490 CI[0.441,0.539] (pass1 0.517); vs hand search 0.487 CI[0.431,0.543] (pass1 0.527); combine vs heuristic 0.515 CI[0.466,0.564] (pass1 0.477). Pass 2 NOT better than pass 1 - moved within noise, all CIs span 0.5. Pearson(value,search-target) rose 0.825->0.904 (fits the search value better) but play did not improve. One expert-iteration loop yields no measurable gain on the simple Mega-Abomasnow deck (its ceiling is parity, consistent with every prior result). Kept pass-1 value as live. Next per user ranking: complex deck.

belief (opp_prior=meta) 32-8 = 0.800 [0.652,0.895]; same-deck (opp_prior=None) 37-3 = 0.925 [0.801,0.974]. Belief did NOT beat same-deck. NOT a refutation -- an adversarial review (dropoff/inbox) confirmed two things from the code: (a) the 1-ply hand-leaf search evaluates the START OF MY NEXT TURN, which barely depends on the opponent's hidden cards (search.py's own docstring concedes this); opp_prior only changes the opponent's single rolled-out reply, gated mostly by their VISIBLE active+energy, so a tie is the EXPECTED null whether or not belief matters; (b) the A/B is UNPAIRED + UNSEEDED, so a plausible ~0.05-0.08 edge is buried in two independent n=40 Wilson CIs. The plumbing is verified correct (deck exclusion picks a real different archetype, opp_prior flows into the opp's sampled hidden cards). Parked, not refuted.

The distilled net is OFFLINE-FAITHFUL but a POOR POLICY. Offline (held-out, canonical eq-class): DISTILL top-1 all 0.570 / tch-dev 0.494 / hi-crit 0.564, vs the chose-option-0 baseline all 0.352 / tch-dev 0.000 -- the net reproduces the search teacher far above the positional prior, and FULL beats no-deltas/no-effects/no-embedding by ~0.03-0.05 on tch-dev (within small-n noise, n=158). BUT in the arena it LOSES badly: standalone agent_rank 0.117 vs heuristic / 0.233 vs first; hybrid (net on strategic only, heuristic else) 0.150 vs heuristic / 0.200 vs first. Anchor: search 0.833 vs heuristic (50-10, n=60; harness sound, search dominates). VERIFIED this is NOT an inference bug -- ranker.predict's features match the trainer's exactly (max abs diff 0.0 over 285 options, 0 card_id mismatches). Cause: covariate shift + imperfect per-decision fidelity -- ~0.5 distill top-1 does not compound into a policy, and self-play drifts off KanNinomiya's training distribution (classic behavior-cloning failure). agent_search (the teacher) remains the strongest agent and the submission.
